name: Conan mac Morna
description: Conan mac Morna, also known as Conan Maol or Conan the Bald, is a prominent figure in the Fenian Cycle of Irish mythology. He is a complex character, often portrayed as a grumpy, cowardly, and somewhat grotesque warrior, yet also possessing moments of surprising cunning and loyalty. He is a member of the Fianna, the band of warriors led by Fionn mac Cumhaill. Conan's physical appearance is frequently described as unattractive, with a bald head and a tendency to complain. Despite his flaws, Conan is an integral part of many Fenian tales, often serving as a foil to the more heroic Fionn and other members of the Fianna. His role is often comedic, providing a source of levity within the more serious narratives. While not a leader or a particularly skilled warrior, Conan's presence adds depth and complexity to the Fenian Cycle, highlighting the diverse personalities within the Fianna and reminding us that even heroes can have their shortcomings. His actions and interactions often drive the plot forward, and his unique perspective provides a valuable contrast to the more idealized portrayals of other Fenian heroes. He is known for his sharp tongue and tendency to speak his mind, even when it's unpopular, adding a layer of realism to the heroic tales. Conan's enduring presence in Irish folklore speaks to the humanizing effect of flawed characters within heroic narratives.
subtitle: Grumpy warrior of the Fianna
achievements: Survived numerous battles with the Fianna
Outsmarted enemies through cunning rather than strength
Provided comic relief in serious situations
Remained loyal to Fionn mac Cumhaill despite his flaws
Contributed to the overall success of the Fianna

keyMyths.title: Conan's Greed
keyMyths.description: Conan's insatiable appetite and desire for the best portions often lead to conflict within the Fianna. This greed frequently puts him in humorous and precarious situations. He often complains about the distribution of food and spoils.

keyMyths.title: Conan's Cowardice
keyMyths.description: Despite being a warrior, Conan is often depicted as cowardly, preferring to avoid danger whenever possible. This perceived cowardice is often a source of amusement for the other members of the Fianna. He often hides during battles.

keyMyths.title: Conan's Cunning
keyMyths.description: While not physically strong, Conan possesses a sharp mind and is often able to outsmart his enemies through cleverness. This cunning often saves the day when brute force fails. He uses his wit to escape dangerous situations.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
